name: Tylwyth Teg
description: The Tylwyth Teg, meaning 'Fair Folk' or 'Beautiful Family', are a supernatural race in Welsh mythology, akin to fairies or elves. They are often depicted as beings of great beauty and power, living in a hidden realm, typically underground or in secluded forests and lakes. Interactions with humans can be both beneficial and perilous, as they are known for their trickery and capricious nature. They are associated with music, dance, and otherworldly feasts, and are believed to have strong connections to the land and the seasons. While sometimes benevolent, offering gifts or assistance, they are also capable of inflicting curses, stealing children, or leading travelers astray. Respect and caution are advised when encountering places believed to be their domain, as their laws and customs differ greatly from those of humans. Stories of the Tylwyth Teg serve as cautionary tales, highlighting the dangers of venturing too far into the unknown and the importance of respecting the boundaries between the mortal and supernatural worlds. They possess magic and live in their own plane of existence, sometimes overlapping with the human world. Their society is structured with its own rules and hierarchies. The Tylwyth Teg are a powerful force in the Welsh mythos, representing the allure and danger of the unseen world.
subtitle: The Fair Folk of Welsh Lore
appearance: The Tylwyth Teg are generally described as exceptionally beautiful, often with fair skin, bright eyes, and flowing hair. They are typically depicted as being of human-like stature, though sometimes smaller and more delicate. Their clothing is often described as being made of natural materials, such as leaves, flowers, or woven moonlight, and they may adorn themselves with jewels and precious metals. Some accounts suggest they can alter their appearance at will, appearing as animals or other creatures to deceive or lure humans.
habitat: The Tylwyth Teg are believed to inhabit hidden realms, such as underground kingdoms, secluded forests, lakes, and caves. They are often associated with places of natural beauty and power, such as ancient standing stones, fairy rings, and sacred groves. Their dwellings are said to be filled with riches and wonders, but are often inaccessible to humans without invitation or trickery.
behavior: The Tylwyth Teg are known for their capricious and unpredictable behavior. They can be generous and helpful to those who treat them with respect, but they are also quick to anger and punish those who offend them. They are fond of music, dance, and revelry, and are often depicted hosting lavish feasts in their hidden realms. They are also known for their trickery, often playing pranks on humans or leading them astray with illusions and enchantments. Interactions with the Tylwyth Teg are always fraught with danger, as their motives are often unclear and their actions can have unforeseen consequences.

keyMyths.title: The Legend of Llyn y Fan Fach
keyMyths.description: A young man marries a maiden from the lake, one of the Tylwyth Teg, but breaks the conditions of their marriage, causing her to return to the lake. Their descendants become renowned physicians.

sources.author: W. Jenkyn Thomas
sources.description: A collection of Welsh fairy tales and folklore.
sources.text: A collection of Welsh fairy tales and folklore.
